subject: French
source: Liberia MOE National Curriculum - French Grades 10-12

curriculum.topic: Les Verbes et Négation (Verbs and Negation)
learning_outcomes: Apply verb-noun agreement in speaking and writing French
Distinguish and identify verb groups in sentences
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
objectives: Define verb in French
Identify and discuss verbs in sentences using French Language
Name the verb groups
Conjugate and construct sentences using the appropriate verb tense in the affirmative and negative forms
key_content: Groups of verbs
Conjugation (er, ir, oire, re) verbs in present, past, future tenses
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: sentence construction (e.g., Mariam mange le riz)
Negation: ne + verb + pas, ne plus, ne jamais
activities: State the importance of different verbs and their usage

learning_outcomes: Build and develop vocabulary skills through the effective use of adjectives
Develop skills with the appropriate use of adjectives through speaking and writing
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
objectives: Define adjective in French
Identify different kinds of adjectives used in sentences to describe persons, places, and things in French
Distinguish the masculine and feminine forms of adjectives used to describe person, place, or thing (color, size, and shape)
key_content: Vocabulary development: petit, grand, gros, méchant
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: describing objects, people, and places (physical, size, structure, shape, state of being)
Être + adjective (masculine and feminine): Il est gros/petit, Elle est grosse/petite
Article + adjective (Nationality): un Chinois, un Libérien, une Libérienne
activities: Write sentences describing and showing possessions of persons
assessment: Students' demonstration describing various objects and persons (physical, structure, state of being, possession)

curriculum.topic: Les Noms et Les Articles (Nouns and Articles)
learning_outcomes: Build and develop vocabulary skills through the effective use of nouns and articles
Develop skills with the appropriate use of nouns and articles through speaking and writing
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
Identify the gender of nouns and articles in sentences
objectives: State the definition of noun and article
Identify and describe nouns and articles in French
Explain nouns and articles
Construct sentences using nouns and articles
key_content: Vocabulary development: le nez, etc.
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: gender of nouns
Articles: definite, indefinite, singular, plural
Example: Le père d'Ali attrappe son cabris
activities: Construct sentences using nouns and articles
Write sentences describing and showing nouns and articles
Identifying nouns and articles in sentences
State important points about the usage of nouns and articles
materials: Textbooks (Mastery for French, Et Vous?, Easy French Step-by-Step)
assessment: Students' demonstration answering questions on activities related to articles and nouns
Debate (boys vs girls) on gender of nouns and articles

learning_outcomes: Build and develop vocabulary skills through the effective use of prepositions
Develop skills with the appropriate use of prepositions through speaking and writing
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
objectives: Identify and describe the use of prepositions in French Language
Explain the definition of preposition
Construct sentences using prepositions
Differentiate the kinds of prepositions
key_content: Vocabulary development: à, avant, avec, devant, pour, derrière, à côté de
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: prepositions of location, time, and sequence
Interrogative + preposition: Qui est-ce dans la maison?
activities: Write sentences describing and showing different kinds of prepositions
assessment: Students' demonstration using maps to show which county/country each student comes from

curriculum.topic: Les Parties du Corps Humain (Parts of the Human Body)
learning_outcomes: Identify, describe, and name parts of the human body in French
Use short conversation in French to describe the human body
Translate words (body parts) from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
Use simple adjectives to describe the parts of the human body
objectives: Describe, identify, and name the different parts of the human body in French
Develop short conversation in French to tell the function of each part of the human body
List the parts of the human body in French
key_content: Vocabulary development: le nez, la main, la tête, le visage, le pied
Grammar: possessive + noun (mon pied, ma tête, la main)
Simple conversation: voici ma main, c'est mon pied, ce sont mes yeux, voici mon genou
activities: Role play using short conversation in French to describe the human body parts
Draw and label the basic parts of the human body
assessment: Class participation and presentation using short conversation in French (boys vs girls)
Illustrate and label each part of the body in French (students' demonstration)
Individual and group assignments and presentations

learning_outcomes: Identify, describe, and list different professions in French
Use short conversation in French to describe each profession and their importance
Translate words (professions) from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
objectives: Describe, identify, and list the different kinds of professions in French
Develop short conversation in French to tell about each profession
Discuss the various kinds of professions in French
key_content: Vocabulary development: musicien, chanteuse, médecin
Grammar: noun + être + article + profession (je suis un médecin, tu es une musicienne)
Simple conversation: nous sommes des chanteurs
activities: Discuss the various professions in French
Role play using short conversation in French to describe each profession and their importance
assessment: Debate (boys vs girls) on the importance of each profession
Individual and group assignments and presentations

curriculum.topic: Les Verbes et Négation (Verbs and Negation)
learning_outcomes: Apply verb-noun agreement in speaking and writing French
Distinguish and identify verb groups in sentences
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
objectives: Identify and describe the verbs in sentences using French Language
Name the verb groups
Conjugate and construct sentences using the appropriate verb tense in the affirmative and negative forms
key_content: Conjugation (er, ir, oire, re) verbs in present, past, imperfect, and future tenses
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: sentence construction and essay writing (subject and verb)
Examples: Mariam mange le riz, René regarde la télé
Negation: ne + verb + pas, ne plus, ne jamais
activities: State the importance of different verbs and their usage

curriculum.topic: Les Adjectifs Comparatifs et Superlatifs (Comparative and Superlative Degree of Adjectives)
learning_outcomes: Build and develop skills through the effective use of comparatives according to their gender and number
Develop skills with the appropriate use of comparatives and superlatives through speaking and writing
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
objectives: Identify, describe, and distinguish the comparative and superlative degree of adjective according to gender and number in French Language
Compare nouns, adjectives, and adverbs using the comparative degree in French
Compare adjectives and adverbs using the superlative degree in French
key_content: Vocabulary development: plus, moins, aussi
Comparative degree: plus...que, moins...que, autant...que
Superlative degree: le plus, la plus, les plus
Examples: Sylvie a plus de frères que toi, Daniel est plus grand que vous, Marie est la plus grande fille
activities: Construct sentences using comparative degree according to gender and number
Write sentences comparing nouns, adjectives, and verbs
Identifying comparative and superlative adjectives in sentences

curriculum.topic: Les Noms et Les Articles (Nouns and Articles)
learning_outcomes: Build and develop vocabulary skills through the effective use of nouns and articles
Develop skills with the appropriate use of nouns and articles according to their gender and number through speaking and writing
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French
objectives: Identify and describe nouns and articles in French Language
Explain nouns and articles and state the definition of each
Construct sentences using nouns and articles
Group nouns according to categories (identifying nouns and articles in sentences)
key_content: Vocabulary development: le riz, un, une banane
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: gender of nouns (un garçon, une fille, des garçons, des filles, un cahier, une chaise)
Example: le cahier est sur la table
Articles: definite, indefinite, singular, plural
Example: Le père d'Ali attrappe son cabris
activities: Construct sentences using nouns and articles
Write sentences describing and showing nouns and articles
Identifying nouns and articles in sentences (genders of nouns)
State the important points about the usage of nouns and articles
materials: Textbooks (Mastery for French, Et Vous?, Easy French Step-by-Step)
assessment: Students' demonstration answering questions on activities related to articles and nouns
Debate (boys vs girls) on gender of nouns and articles

learning_outcomes: Build and develop vocabulary skills through the effective use of prepositions
Develop skills with the appropriate use of prepositions through speaking and writing
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
objectives: Identify and describe the use of prepositions in French Language
Explain the definition of preposition
Construct sentences using prepositions
Differentiate the kinds of prepositions
key_content: Vocabulary development: qui, quoi
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: prepositions of location, time, and sequence
Interrogative + preposition (qui and quoi)
activities: Write sentences describing and showing different kinds of prepositions
materials: Textbooks (Mastery for French, Et Vous?, Easy French Step-by-Step)
assessment: Students' demonstration using maps to show which county/country each student comes from

curriculum.topic: Les Verbes et Négation (Verbs and Negation)
learning_outcomes: Apply verb-noun agreement in speaking and writing French
Distinguish and identify verb groups in sentences
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
objectives: Identify and describe the verbs in sentences using French Language
Name the verb groups
Conjugate and construct sentences using the appropriate verb tense in the affirmative and negative forms
key_content: Conjugation (er, ir, oire, re) verbs in present, past, imperfect, and future tenses
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: sentence construction and essay writing (subject and verb)
Examples: Mariam mange le riz, René regarde la télé
Negation: ne + verb + pas, ne plus, ne jamais
activities: State the importance of different verbs and their usage

learning_outcomes: Build and develop vocabulary skills through the effective use of descriptive words
Develop skills with the appropriate use of adjectives through speaking and writing
Translate sentences from French to English or English to French (oral and written)
objectives: Identify and discuss the different kinds of adjectives to describe persons, places, and things in French Language
Distinguish the masculine and feminine forms of adjectives to modify a person, place, or thing using possessive words
key_content: Vocabulary development: petit, grand, gros, méchant
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: describing objects, people, and places (physical, size, structure, shape, state of being)
Être + adjective (masculine and feminine): Il est gros/petit, Elle est grosse/petite
Kinds of adjectives: demonstrative, possessive, descriptive, indefinite, interrogative (agreement and position)
activities: Write sentences describing and showing possessions of persons
assessment: Students' demonstration describing various objects, persons (physical, structure, state of being, possession, gender, abilities, learning styles)

Explain nouns and articles and state the definition of each
Construct sentences using nouns and articles
Group nouns according to categories (identifying nouns and articles in sentences)
key_content: Vocabulary development: le nez, un cahier, une fille
Oral and written expressions in French
Grammar: gender of nouns singular and plural (un garçon, une fille, des garçons, des filles, un cahier, une chaise)
Example: un cahier est sur la table

Example: Le père d'Ali attrappe son cabris

Write sentences describing and showing nouns and articles
Identifying nouns and articles in sentences (genders of nouns)
State the important points about the usage of nouns and articles

assessment: Students' demonstration answering questions on activities related to articles and nouns
Debate (boys vs girls) on gender of nouns and articles
